rationale: Both correctly flag PR #4834 as the likely trigger (matching yenuo26's comment) and propose a plausible level=2→level=1 test fix, but the actual thread shows the issue is unresolved — Flink-ddd (the assignee) is asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI cluster run, not confirming a fix or closure, which both candidates wrongly assert. Y is more confidently wrong ('already in main, keep closed') and fabricates extensive unverifiable specifics (exact PR title, merge date, three named regression tests, line-ranged code snippets, 'Repro A/B/C' labels) presented as established fact, which hurts grounding more than it helps. X hedges more honestly (close/keep-open conditional on CI confirmation, explicit reopen condition) and sticks closer to verifiable content from the issue body itself, making it marginally less overconfident despite similar overall miss on the true unresolved status.
